name: Camino del Cid – Calamocha and Monreal del Campo
name_original: Camino del Cid – Calamocha y Monreal del Campo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Calamocha offers diverse landscapes across the extensive Jiloca plain in Aragon, Spain. The region is characterized by the flat expanse of the Jiloca plain, through which the Jiloca River flows, providing picturesque routes. Many routes utilize the Santander–Mediterranean Greenway, a conditioned path on a former railway line, offering well-maintained surfaces for cyclists. This area combines natural features with historical attractions, making it suitable for various cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Calamocha The most popular touring cycling route is Camino del Cid – Calamocha and Monreal del Campo, an easy 20.3 miles (32.6 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 53 minutes to complete, following a historic path. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail, a moderate 19.2 miles (30.9 km) path. This route circles the notable Laguna de Gallocanta, offering views of the natural reserve. Local touring cyclists also love the The Three Taifas Trail, a 16.6 miles (26.8 km) trail leading through the Jiloca plain, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Calamocha is defined by the flat Jiloca plain, the winding Jiloca River, and the Santander–Mediterranean Greenway. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Calamocha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a handful of reviews. More than dozens of touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Calamocha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Calamocha?
A: Calamocha offers nearly 20 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 6 easy, 4 moderate, and 9 difficult options to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Calamocha?
A: Yes, Calamocha features 6 easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957109847/camino-del-cid-calamocha-and-monreal-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid – Calamocha and Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 20.3-mile (32.6 km) trail that follows a historic path.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is defined by the flat expanse of the Jiloca plain and the winding Jiloca River, offering picturesque routes. A unique natural attraction nearby is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, a spring creating a spectacular karst relief with continuously falling water, forming stone and moss walls and small caves.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists, there are 9 difficult routes. One notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Ámbar – Huesa del Común loop from Mas de Teller&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 84 miles (135 km) with significant elevation changes, offering a substantial challenge.

Q: What historical attractions can be visited while cycling in Calamocha?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Calamocha offers historical points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge of Luco de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient structure that was part of a Roman road. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calamocha&lt;/a&gt; itself has historical significance as a former fortified enclave.

Q: Are there any loop trails for touring cyclists in Calamocha?
A: Yes, several loop trails are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956708468/laguna-de-gallocanta-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 19.2-mile (30.9 km) path that circles the notable Laguna de Gallocanta, offering views of the natural reserve.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Calamocha?
A: The touring cycling routes around Calamocha are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the flat Jiloca plain to routes utilizing the well-maintained &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4592106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes that follow former railway lines?
A: Yes, a major draw for touring cyclists in Calamocha is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4592106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. This conditioned path utilizes a former railway line, providing well-maintained surfaces and a unique way to explore the region.

Q: Can I find moderate touring cycling routes in Calamocha?
A: Certainly. Calamocha offers 4 moderate touring cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Anento&lt;/a&gt;, a 18.5-mile (29.8 km) route with some elevation changes, providing a good balance for intermediate riders.

Q: What is the general landscape like for touring cycling in Calamocha?
A: The landscape for touring cycling in Calamocha is primarily characterized by the extensive Jiloca plain, offering mostly flat terrain ideal for relaxed rides. The Jiloca River winds through this plain, providing scenic riverside paths. However, some routes venture into areas with more significant elevation changes for those seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through charming villages?
A: Many routes in the Calamocha region traverse charming villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956698744/the-three-taifas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Three Taifas Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads through the Jiloca plain, connecting various points of interest and likely passing through local settlements, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5058102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monreal del Campo&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: From El Poyo del Cid you have an excellent view over the Jiloca Valley. The Castilian knight El Cid had set up camp here during his forays during the Reconquista - hence the name of the place. A statue was erected in his honor at the end of the town.

Monreal del Campo is a fascinating place where you can learn all about the cultivation and harvesting of saffron. Located in the Plaza Mayor, the museum offers an interactive experience with traditional tools, historical photographs and workshops for the whole family.🌺
